name: Oasis Of Hope Cdc
description_short: 501(c)(3) non-profit CDC offering HUD-certified housing counseling, foreclosure prevention, and homebuyer education workshops to low-to-moderate income families in South Florida.
description_long:
Oasis of Hope is a 501(c)(3) community development corporation established in 2009 in Pompano Beach, Florida, under the leadership of founder and CEO Jacqueline Reed-Stills. The organization serves low- and moderate-income individuals and families across Broward, Palm Beach, and Miami-Dade counties with a mission to provide basic community housing services and increase access to decent, safe, and affordable housing.

The organization offers HUD-certified housing counseling, foreclosure prevention services, homebuyer education workshops, rental assistance, utility assistance, and homeowner assistance programs. They provide free educational resources and workshops designed to help clients understand the homebuying process, financial literacy (including a Gen Z Smart Money 101 workshop), and pathways to homeownership. Their team includes multiple HUD-Certified Housing Counselors and a dedicated intake specialist to guide clients through available programs.

Oasis of Hope distinguishes itself through direct partnerships with major lenders like Wells Fargo, which expands financing options for their clients. They emphasize personalized, family-oriented support and have recently expanded services to help young adults transition into independent living through affordable housing programs. The organization also actively engages the community through volunteer opportunities in workshops, marketing, and youth programming.

As a non-profit housing counselor organization, Oasis of Hope is legitimate and well-positioned for consumers seeking free housing guidance. The primary limitation is geographic scope—services are concentrated in specific South Florida counties—and the website does not detail wait times, counselor availability, or specific assistance amounts for their utility and rental support programs.
pros: HUD-certified housing counselors on staff (Edgar Palencia, Christina Malcolm, Phillys Brown)
Completely free educational workshops and housing counseling services
Verified 501(c)(3) non-profit status with transparent mission and leadership
Partnerships with major lenders (Wells Fargo) to expand client financing options
Specialized foreclosure prevention services to help homeowners keep their homes
Multiple service offerings beyond counseling: utility assistance, rental assistance, homeowner assistance
Youth-focused programs including Gen Z financial literacy workshops and young adult housing transition support
cons: Geographic limitation: services only available in Broward, Palm Beach, and Miami-Dade counties
Website lacks specific details on assistance amounts, approval rates, or typical counseling timelines
No information about waitlists or current case load capacity
Limited transparency on which specific foreclosure prevention outcomes are achievable
Volunteer intake form requires submission with no clear next steps or timeline communicated
best_for: South Florida homeowners at risk of foreclosure seeking free prevention counseling
First-time homebuyers in Broward, Palm Beach, or Miami-Dade counties wanting education and lender connections
Low-to-moderate income renters needing utility or rental assistance programs
Young adults (Gen Z and beyond) seeking affordable housing and financial literacy education
diagnosis: Oasis of Hope is best for South Florida residents (Broward, Palm Beach, Miami-Dade counties) seeking free, legitimate HUD-certified housing counseling and foreclosure prevention assistance. The main caveat is that services are geographically restricted and the website lacks specific details on assistance amounts, timelines, and approval outcomes.
services: Financial literacy workshops (Gen Z Smart Money 101)
Housing pathway workshops (Miami's Pathway to Homeownership)
Young adult independent living transition support
Community volunteer and youth engagement programs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 600 Sw 3Rd St, Ste 2290, Pompano Beach, FL 33060
meta_description: Non-profit CDC offering HUD-certified housing counseling, foreclosure prevention, and homebuyer education in South Florida.
